title: Icon for State
text: Icon for State Specify the icons used to represent the different states of the current tree item type at run time. The Role (Node) property determines the states the item can have. Select the type of icon ( Glyph for a Uniface glyph or Bitmap for any type of supported image file). if you choose, Bitmap, you can also seEnter an image name or select an image. Table 1. Tree Item States State Condition Normal Item is not open and not selected. Selected Item is selected in the List View (if specified). Open Node is open in the Tree View, but not selected. Open and Selected Node is open in the Tree View and selected. The icon for Normal state is mandatory. If no other icons are specified, the icon for Normal state is used. If an icon is specified for Normal and for Selected, but not for Open, the Select icon is used. For best results, and following the convention used in Microsoft Windows Explorer, use the same icon for Normal and Selected (a closed folder, for example) and another icon for Open and Open/Selected (an open folder, for example). Note: The width, height, and color-depth of all icons are determined by the image specified by the Normal icon of the first tree item Type. Any other icon that does not conform to these three values is discarded and will not be used in the widget. Related Links Type Determines the role of tree items, and specifies the icons that are used to represent these tree items at run time. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
